instruction: Answer each trivia question — True or False?

items.question: The Sahara Desert is the largest desert in the world.
items.explanation: Antarctica is the largest desert by area.

items.question: Honey never spoils if stored properly.
items.explanation: Archaeologists found 3000-year-old honey still edible in Egyptian tombs.

items.question: The human body has 206 bones.
items.explanation: Adults have 206 bones; babies are born with about 270.

items.question: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
items.explanation: Tall structures like the Empire State Building are struck dozens of times per year.

items.question: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system.
items.explanation: Venus has a thick atmosphere causing extreme greenhouse effect (~465°C).

items.question: Goldfish have a 3-second memory.
items.explanation: Goldfish can remember things for months.

items.question: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
items.explanation: It is too narrow to be seen without aid from orbit.

items.question: Octopuses have three hearts.
items.explanation: Two pump blood to the gills; one pumps to the rest of the body.

items.question: Bananas are technically berries.
items.explanation: Botanically, bananas qualify as berries while strawberries don't.

items.question: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain measured from base to peak.
items.explanation: Mauna Kea in Hawaii is taller measured from its base on the ocean floor.

items.question: Water can boil and freeze at the same time under the right conditions.
items.explanation: This happens at the triple point of water.

items.question: Bats are blind.
items.explanation: Bats can see; many species have excellent night vision.

items.question: A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
items.explanation: Venus rotates so slowly that one day is about 243 Earth days, while its year is 225.

items.question: Humans use only 10% of their brains.
items.explanation: Brain scans show activity across the entire brain for various tasks.

items.question: Sharks are older than trees.
items.explanation: Sharks appeared about 400 million years ago; trees about 350 million years ago.

items.question: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights was adopted in 1948.
items.explanation: It was proclaimed by the UN General Assembly on 10 December 1948.
